
```{r}
################################################################################
# PROJECT: Vimentin Disassembly is a Structural Prerequisite for Enucleation in iPSC-Derived Erythrocytes: Insights from Avian Erythropoiesis
# SCRIPT:  03_Validation_of_Avian_Erythroid_Stages.R
#Public Bulk RNA-seq validation of scRNA-defined erythroid stages
# 
# PURPOSE: Validation of scRNA-defined erythroid stages using independent public bulk RNA-seq datasets.
#
# DATA SOURCES:
#   1. Richard et al. (2016) - Chicken T2EC (Progenitors vs. Differentiated)
#   2. NCBI SRA PRJNA305527 - Immature Erythrocytes
#   3. NCBI SRA PRJNA445563 - Mature Erythrocytes
################################################################################

# --- 1. Load All Required Libraries ---
library(Seurat)      # Single-cell analysis
library(tidyverse)   # Data wrangling (dplyr, ggplot2, tidyr)
library(edgeR)       # Bulk RNA-seq normalization
library(pheatmap)    # Correlation heatmaps
library(openxlsx)    # Exporting result tables

# --- 2. Directory Setup ---
# Set working directory to the project root (if not using Rproj)
# setwd("path/to/repository") 
if (!dir.exists("./Results")) dir.create("./Results")
if (!dir.exists("./Processed_Objects")) dir.create("./Processed_Objects")
if (!dir.exists("./External_Data_File")) dir.create("./External_Data_File")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 3A: PREPARE SCRNA-SEQ REFERENCE (AVERAGE EXPRESSION)
# ==============================================================================

# 3. Load the Integrated Avian Atlas (from Script 02)
# We use the integrated object because it contains the full developmental spectrum from marrow progenitors to terminal blood maturity.
chicken <- readRDS("./Processed_Objects/BMscRNA_PBscRNA.rds")

# Ensure RNA assay is used for bulk-to-single-cell comparison
chicken <- NormalizeData(chicken, assay = "RNA")

# Set standard identities and order
Idents(chicken) <- "seurat_clusters"
new_cluster_names_chicken <- c(
  "0" = "Late polychromatic blast", "1" = "Mid polychromatic blast",
  "2" = "Late erythroblast", "3" = "Erythroid progenitor 2",
  "4" = "Early polychromatic blast", "5" = "Erythroid progenitor 1",
  "6" = "Immature erythrocyte", "8" = "Early erythroblast",
  "9" = "Mature erythrocyte-C"
)
chicken <- RenameIdents(chicken, new_cluster_names_chicken)
custom_order_chicken <- c(
  "Mature erythrocyte-C", "Immature erythrocyte", "Late polychromatic blast", 
  "Mid polychromatic blast", "Early polychromatic blast", "Late erythroblast", 
  "Early erythroblast", "Erythroid progenitor 2", "Erythroid progenitor 1"
)
Idents(chicken) <- factor(Idents(chicken), levels = custom_order_chicken)

# 4. Generate Pseudobulk Profiles
chicken_avg_list <- AverageExpression(chicken, assays = "RNA", slot = "data")
chicken_avg_exp_df <- as.data.frame(chicken_avg_list$RNA)

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 3B: BONE MARROW VALIDATION (RICHARD ET AL. 2016)
# ==============================================================================

# 5. Load and Normalize Richard 2016 Bulk Data
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SOURCE: Richard et al. (2016), PLOS Biology (DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.1002585).
# BIO-CONTEXT: Chicken T2EC erythroid progenitors (LM1: Self-renewal; DM17: 48h Differentiation).
# 
# GENERATION/RE-ANNOTATION LOGIC:
# The original dataset (OSF: matrix_2793.txt) utilizes legacy Ensembl v4 identifiers. 
# To ensure compatibility with modern scRNA-seq annotations, a custom re-annotation 
# pipeline was implemented:
#   1. Transcript sequences from Ensembl v4 were aligned to Ensembl v7 using BLASTn.
#   2. High-confidence alignments were used to map legacy IDs to official Gene Symbols.
#   3. This process produced the 'Richard2016_Bulk_T2EC.csv' reference used here.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure the re-annotated file is placed in your local directory structure
rna_data <- read.csv("./Raw_Data/External_Data_File/Richard2016_Bulk_T2EC.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Filter for rows that have a Symbol.
rna_data_filtered <- rna_data[!is.na(rna_data$Symbol) & rna_data$Symbol != "", ]
# Remove duplicated gene symbols.
rna_data_filtered <- rna_data_filtered[!duplicated(rna_data_filtered$Symbol), ]
# Prepare count matrix for edgeR normalization (LM1 and DM17 conditions)
count_matrix <- rna_data_filtered[, c("AT276_LM1", "JV47_LM1", "AT276_DM17", "JV47_DM17")]
rownames(count_matrix) <- rna_data_filtered$Symbol

# 6. edgeR Log-CPM Normalization
dge <- DGEList(counts = count_matrix)
keep <- filterByExpr(dge)
dge <- dge[keep, , keep.lib.sizes = FALSE]
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge)
logCPM <- cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)
logCPM_df <- as.data.frame(logCPM)

# Average biological replicates (AT276 & JV47) for final correlation
logCPM_df$LM1 <- rowMeans(logCPM_df[, c("AT276_LM1", "JV47_LM1")])
logCPM_df$DM17 <- rowMeans(logCPM_df[, c("AT276_DM17", "JV47_DM17")])
chicken_avg_exp_df$Symbol <- rownames(chicken_avg_exp_df)
logCPM_df$Symbol <- rownames(logCPM_df)
chicken_avg_exp_df$Symbol <- toupper(chicken_avg_exp_df$Symbol)
logCPM_df$Symbol <- toupper(logCPM_df$Symbol)

# 7. Pearson Correlation Analysis
merged_data <- inner_join(chicken_avg_exp_df, logCPM_df, by = "Symbol")
if (nrow(merged_data) == 0) {
  stop("Merge failed. Please double-check 'head(rownames(chicken))' and 'head(rna_data$Symbol)' to confirm their formats are identical.")
} else {
  cat("Success! Merged data contains", nrow(merged_data), "common genes.\n")
}

# Save Comparison Results 
output_dir <- "." 
cat("\n--- Saving Merged Data for Supplementary Table 4 ---\n")
supp_table_s4_filename <- file.path(output_dir, "./Results/Supplementary Table 4.xlsx")
write.xlsx(merged_data, file = supp_table_s4_filename, rowNames = FALSE, overwrite = TRUE)
cat("Supplementary Table 4 saved successfully to:", supp_table_s4_filename, "\n")

# Visualize Correlation Heatmap
sc_cluster_cols <- custom_order_chicken
bulk_sample_cols <- c("LM1", "DM17") 
expression_data <- merged_data[, c(sc_cluster_cols, bulk_sample_cols)]
cor_matrix <- cor(expression_data[, sc_cluster_cols], expression_data[, bulk_sample_cols], method = "pearson")
# Save the final heatmap.
pdf("./Results/Figure-2D.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
pheatmap(
  cor_matrix,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,  
  cluster_cols = FALSE,  
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#9ECAE1", "#FFFFFF", "#F4B6AE"))(100),
  main = "Correlation: scRNA Clusters vs Averaged Bulk Samples",
  fontsize = 10,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  fontsize_col = 12,
  fontsize_number = 10,     # <-- increase numbers inside tiles
  angle_col = 0,
  display_numbers = TRUE, number_format = "%.2f"
)
dev.off()

cat("Analysis complete. The final, validated plot has been saved.\n")

# ==============================================================================
# SCRIPT 3C: TERMINAL MATURATION VALIDATION (POLARIZED ERYTHROCYTES)
# ==============================================================================

# 8. Load and Process Polarized Bulk Data (Immature vs. Mature)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# # 8. Load and Process Polarized Bulk Data (Immature vs. Mature)
# SOURCE: Immature (SRR2983618; PRJNA305527) & Mature (SRR6893554; PRJNA445563).
# PROCESSING: FASTQs quantified via Salmon (quasi-mapping) and summarized to 
# gene-level symbols via the tximport R package.
#
# PROCESSING RATIONALE:
# Raw FASTQ files were quantified using Salmon in quasi-mapping mode against 
# the G. gallus transcriptome. Transcript abundances were summarized to 
# gene-level counts via the 'tximport' R package. Custom mapping files were 
# used to aggregate counts into Ensembl IDs and official Gene Symbols.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("--- Part 1: Processing Bulk RNA-seq Data ---\n")
mature_path <- "./Raw_Data/External_Data_File/Mature_Ery.csv"
immature_path <- "./Raw_Data/External_Data_File/Immature_Ery.csv"
mature_df <- read.csv(mature_path)
immature_df <- read.csv(immature_path)
colnames(mature_df) <- c("Gene_ID", "Gene", "Mature")
colnames(immature_df) <- c("Gene_ID", "Gene", "Immature")

# Merge conditions and perform Library Size Normalization (logCPM)
combined_bulk <- full_join(mature_df, immature_df, by = c("Gene_ID", "Gene"))
combined_bulk <- combined_bulk %>%
  mutate(
    Mature = ifelse(is.na(Mature), 0, Mature),
    Immature = ifelse(is.na(Immature), 0, Immature)
  )
lib_size_mature <- sum(combined_bulk$Mature)
lib_size_immature <- sum(combined_bulk$Immature)
bulk_norm_data <- combined_bulk %>%
  mutate(
    Mature_logCPM = log2(((Mature / lib_size_mature) * 1e6) + 1),
    Immature_logCPM = log2(((Immature / lib_size_immature) * 1e6) + 1),
    Gene = toupper(trimws(Gene))  # <--- ADD THIS LINE HERE
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Gene, Mature_logCPM, Immature_logCPM) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Gene) & Gene != "")

# 9. Pearson Correlation Analysis (Figure 2E)
cat("\n--- Part 2: Merging data using existing Average Expression ---\n")

# RATIONALE: We do NOT reload the Seurat object. 
# We reuse 'chicken_avg_exp_df' which was created in Section 4.

# Standardize the Single-Cell Gene names to Uppercase
# (Matches the uppercase conversion you did for bulk data in Section 8)
chicken_avg_exp_df$Symbol <- toupper(trimws(rownames(chicken_avg_exp_df))) 

# Merge Data
# FIX: Use 'chicken_avg_exp_df' and 'bulk_norm_data' (the names you actually used)
merged_terminal <- inner_join(chicken_avg_exp_df, bulk_norm_data, by = c("Symbol" = "Gene"))

if (nrow(merged_terminal) == 0) {
  stop("Merge failed for Terminal comparison. Check Gene Symbol formatting.")
} else {
  cat("Success! Merged terminal data contains", nrow(merged_terminal), "common genes.\n")
}

# Calculate Correlation and Plot Heatmap
# Select columns based on names in merged_terminal
cor_matrix_terminal <- cor(
  merged_terminal[, custom_order_chicken], 
  merged_terminal[, c("Immature_logCPM", "Mature_logCPM")], 
  method = "pearson"
)
colnames(cor_matrix_terminal) <- c("Bulk Immature", "Bulk Mature")

# Visualize Correlation Heatmap (Figure 2E)
pdf("./Results/Figure-2E.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
pheatmap(
  cor_matrix_terminal,
  cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_cols = FALSE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#9ECAE1", "#FFFFFF", "#F4B6AE"))(100),
  main = "Correlation: scRNA vs Bulk Erythrocytes",
  display_numbers = TRUE, number_format = "%.2f",
  fontsize = 10, angle_col = 0
)
dev.off()

cat("\nValidation Analysis Complete. Figures 2D, 2E generated in ./Results/\n")
```
